rulings.Case Title: ALI KIA SHABAHANGI et al VS. NADER SHABAHANGI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues/ Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 27, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI AND AGESONG, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES. Defendants' Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The first two causes of action for breach of contract and fraud in the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") set forth two distinct wrongs under each cause of action - 1) the cancellation of Synergy LLC and 2) the refusal to perform under the Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") and Spirit Letter. Pursuant to Lilienthal & Fowler v. Sup. Ct. (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1848, 1854-55, the Court will treat each distinct wrong as a separate cause of action for purposes of this motion. As to the causes of action for breach of contract and fraud as they pertain to the cancellation of Synergy LLC, summary adjudication is granted. Res judicata bars these claims, as they were explicitly addressed and adjudicated by the Final Award. (Declaration of Jennifer Stark, Ex. K, Final Award, p. 9.) As to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, summary adjudication is granted because res judicata bars the entire cause of action. (Id.) Summary adjudication is granted as to the fourth cause of action for tortious interference with contractual relationship. The undisputed evidence establishes that Plaintiffs are not holders of the bonds, are not express beneficiaries, and are not individual signatories to the agreements governing the bonds. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 81-110.) Accordingly, Plaintiffs do not have standing to maintain this cause of action in their individual capacities. As to the cause of action for fraud as it pertains to the refusal to perform under the MOU and Spirit Letter, summary adjudication is denied. Defendant did not meet his initial burden. As to the statute of limitations argument, Defendant does not submit any evidence about when the Plaintiffs discovered the fraud or any evidence about when Defendant's obligations under the MOU and Spirit Letter were triggered. (See UMF 69-80.) As to the argument that the fraud claim is meritless, Defendant does not present any evidence that Plaintiff cannot establish this cause of action. (See UMF 69-80.) A defendant moving for summary judgment on the grounds that an element of a cause of action cannot be established must "present evidence, and not simply point out, that the plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence." (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 854-55.) Because Defendant did not meet his initial burden as to the fraud cause of action, summary adjudication is denied as to the fifth cause of action for Violation of Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200 and the claim for punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: ALI SHABAHANGI VS. NADER SHABAHANGI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 27, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT NADER SHABAHANGI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. As to the statute of limitations argument, there is a triable issue of material fact as to when the alleged breach occurred. Plaintiff states that he received a payment from Defendant in late December 2010. (Declaration of Ali Shabahangi, par. 8.) This statement does not contradict Plaintiff's response to Defendant's request for production. A reasonable jury could find that no transaction record exists for a number of reasons. As to the statute of frauds argument, the critical inquiry is whether by the contract's own terms it would be impossible to perform within a year. (Hollywood Motion Picture Equipment Co. v. Furer (1940) 16 Cal.2d 184, 187.) Under either party's characterization of how long the agreement was supposed to last (as set forth in Undisputed Material Fact 38), it would not have been impossible for the contract to be performed within one year. For example, had Plaintiff sold the property within one year after Defendant re-conveyed it to him, the contract would have been fully performed in less than one year. rulings.Case Title: BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN (BATROA) FOR THE et al VS. ROBERT CROSS AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion (1) For Terminating Sanctions; And (2) For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 27, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANTS ROBERT CROSS AND SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP'S Motion (1) For Terminating Sanctions; And (2) For Monetary Sanctions. Granted in part and denied in part. Granted in part. Defendants are awarded $2500 in sanctions payable no later than May 15, 2015. If plaintiffs do not provide further responses as required by the February 11, 2015 order on or before May 15, 2015, then defendants may make an ex parte application for terminating sanctions in department 613 with proof of plaintiffs' failure to so comply and, upon presentation of such proof and the absence of a satisfactory excuse, terminating sanctions will be granted against plaintiffs. The reduction in the amount of the sanctions requested reflects the fact that this was a very simple motion that could have been filed and heard with only a few hours of work by defense counsel. The Court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOCELYN CRAWFORD-BROWN ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND VS. DIGNITY HEALTH, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders: Compelling Further Responses To Srogs And Rpds
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 27, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOCELYN CRAWFORD-BROWN, ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC's Motion For Orders Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories and Production Of Documents to Defendant Dignity Health. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The plaintiffs' motion is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The motion is DENIED in regard to Special Interrogatory Nos. 18-21 and Request for Production Nos. 15 & 16 as overbroad and overly burdensome. The motion is GRANTED as to Request for Production Nos. 24-27. Both parties are reminded to comply with Local Rule 8.2 in regard to lodging of courtesy copies of moving and opposition papers with the Law and Motion Department. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY D. CHU VS. LEAP TRANSIT, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify And Order For The Appointment Of Independent Counsel For Corporate Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 27, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF JEFFREY CHU'S Motion To Disqualify And Order For The Appointment Of Independent Counsel For Corporate Defendant. Denied provided that defendants provide proof at the hearing of a waiver of a potential or actual conflict in compliance with CRPC 3-310(C) and 3-600(E). None of plaintiffs' allegations necessarily result in actual conflict between the individual defendants and the corporate defendant and, in all events, any conflict that does exist or could arise may be waived per CRPC 3-310(C) and 3-600(E).
